div1.entryFree:
Cage, 1) an enclosure to keep birds in: Ado I, 3, 36. As III, 2, 389. Tit. III, 1, 84. Lr. V, 3, 9. Cymb. III, 3, 42.
2) a prison for petty malefactors: his father had never a house but the c. H6B IV, 2, 56.
3) wicker-work, basket: I must up-fill this osier c. of ours with baleful weeds, Rom. II, 3, 7.
